task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WordPress native 'Edit Image' screen or media details for 'Massive Landscape', the image dimensions should show a width of exactly 1200 pixels (e.g., in the 'Scale Image' input fields or the 'Dimensions' text).

In the WordPress native 'Edit Image' screen for 'Sideways Portrait', the image should appear rotated 90 degrees clockwise, OR there should be evidence of the agent clicking the 'Rotate right' button. Note: The image preview may appear broken due to a known environment defect; accept clear GUI attempts to rotate it.
In the attachment details or block settings for 'Sideways Portrait', the 'Alternative Text' input field must contain exactly 'Professional headshot restored'.

task_completion.description: Create a new post titled 'Meet the Team'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress post editor (Gutenberg) is open with the title field set to exactly 'Meet the Team'.

task_completion.description: Include both modified images in the new post
task_completion.visual_evidence: The content area of the 'Meet the Team' post contains both the 'Massive Landscape' and 'Sideways Portrait' images (even if the portrait renders as a broken image icon).
A 'Post published' notification is visible, or the post settings panel clearly shows the status as 'Published' (not Draft).

integrity.description: Agent used the native WordPress GUI to edit images
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress 'Edit Image' interface being used. There should be no terminal windows open using ImageMagick, WP-CLI, or other command-line tools to manipulate the images.

integrity.description: Agent did not use external image editing software
integrity.visual_evidence: No external applications like GIMP, Photoshop, or web-based image editors should be visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: Agent modified existing media rather than uploading new files
integrity.visual_evidence: The WordPress Media Library should not show newly uploaded duplicate images. The agent must be editing the pre-existing 'Massive Landscape' and 'Sideways Portrait' attachments.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected landscape width is exactly 1200 pixels. Expected portrait alt text is exactly 'Professional headshot restored'. Expected post title is exactly 'Meet the Team'. Note: Due to an environment setup defect, the 'Sideways Portrait' file is actually an invalid HTML file instead of a JPEG. It will likely appear as a broken image icon in WordPress. Evaluate the agent based on its interaction with the WordPress UI (e.g., clicking the rotate button, setting the alt text, inserting it into the post) even if the image preview fails to render or the rotation fails server-side.
